title: RSS 2.0, Atom 1.0, Namespaces, and Extension Policy · Execution Trace: Feed Go Extension Policy
summary: The parser assumes RSS only, ignores Atom namespaces, treats optional fields as required, loses xml:base and language, and guesses extension meaning. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go extension policy increment in standards-aware syndication vocabulary map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Allowlist interpreted extensions, bound ignored markup, and surface unsupported constructs for review.
Admit documented date and media-type variants through explicit parsers and retain original values on rejection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Extension Policy · Theory Feed Go Extension Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 7384 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove RSS and Atom format semantics, expanded names, variance, and extension restraint; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
